titleStmt.title: Letter from Thomas Kirkpatrick to Charles Molyneux Benson, 5 February 1917
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Thomas Kirkpatrick

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
From 1910 until 1954 Thomas Percy Kirkpatrick (1869-1954) served as the registrar for the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland. He also served as the general secretary of the Royal Academy of Medicine in Ireland. He took a particular interest in what were then termed venereal diseases; to encourage his patients (many of whom were prostitutes) to attend, he held a clinic for women at a discreet early morning hour to facilitate anonymity.
Charles Molyneaux Benson (1877-1919) was a registered fellow of the Royal College of Surgeons. In 1916 he was working in Sir Patrick Dunn's Hospital.

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.placeName: Royal College of Physicians, Kildare Street, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
5th FEBRUARY 17. C.M. Benson, Esq., M.D.,
Secretary of Council,
R.C.S.I. Re College of Nursing Conference Committee. Dear Sir, I am directed by the President and Fellows of this
College to forward you a copy of the following resolution
adopted at a meeting of the College held on Friday the 2nd
instant : Resolved : "that the Conference Committee be requested to
submit to the Colleges a scheme for the promotion of the
uniformity of the curriculum and the training of Irish
Nurses, either in the connection with, or independent of, the
British College of Nursing, whichever may seem to the
Committee the more desirable course to adopt.
The Royal College of Physicians are most anxious that
such a scheme should have the approval of Irish Nurses
and in view of this the President and Fellows would
suggest to the Committee the advisableness of consulting
with Irish Nursing authorities on the matter." I am,
Yours faithfully,
Fellow & Registrar.
